title: On Drawings Of The Statues Of Apollo, Venus, And Hercules, Made For Pope By Sir Godfrey Kneller.
content:
What god, what genius did the pencil move,
    When Kneller painted these?
    'Twas friendship, warm as Phoebus, kind as Love,
    And strong as Hercules.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alexander Pope, titled "On Drawings Of The Statues Of Apollo, Venus, And Hercules, Made For Pope By Sir Godfrey Kneller.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
